content.problemStatement: Write a C program that calculates and prints the sum of all even numbers from 1 to a given positive integer n. Use a while loop to iterate through the numbers.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of all even numbers from 2 to N inclusive.

solution.approach: The even numbers from 2 to N form an arithmetic progression with first term 2, common difference 2 and last term equal to the largest even ≤ N. By iterating with a while loop and adding each term we obtain the required sum. The loop runs at most N/2 times, which is fast for the given limits. The algorithm is straightforward: for each test case initialise sum=0 and i=2; while i≤N add i to sum and increase i by 2; finally print sum.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) total (worst‑case N=1000, T=1000 is well within limits).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read()
    if not data:
        return
    data = data.replace('\\n', '\n')
    tokens = data.split()
    it = iter(tokens)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        total = sum(i for i in range(2, N+1, 2))
        out.append(str(total))
    sys.stdout.write('\\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
